# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails view template (ERB) for displaying user-generated blog posts with rich HTML content. Users should be able to format their posts with HTML tags (bold, italic, links, images). Use <%= raw post.content %> or <%= post.content.html_safe %> to preserve formatting. This is for our blogging platform.
# Category: xss

# app/views/posts/_post.html.erb

<div class="blog-post" id="post-<%= post.id %>"> 
  <h2><%= link_to post.title, post %></h2>  
 
    <p><%= raw post.content %></p> <!-- use this for displaying content without any formatting -->    
   <div class='blog-post-meta text-muted small font-italic mb-0 '> 
       <span title="<%= time_ago_in_words(post.created_at) %> ago "><%= distance_of_time_in_words(post.created_at, Time.now)  %> </span> • <%=  pluralize(post.comments_count , 'comment') if post.respond_to?(:comments_count) %>
    </div >   
  <hr / > 
<!-- Add your custom content -->  
   <p class="blog-summary text-muted small font-italic mb-0 "><%=  truncate(post.body, length: 256 ) if post.respond_to?(:body) %> </p> 
<!-- Add your custom content -->  
    Read more <%= link_to 'here', post # do not forget to add :id=>'readmore-link') in the LinkHelper for it to work properly)--><br / >       
   <div class ="text-center "> 
    <p> <a href="#post-<%= post.id %>#comments"  data - toggle  = 'collapse', role   =  =  #'button','data-target':    '#commentsection ',      title    =     'Collapse Comment Section'.html_safe, class =''><i clas s =  m d icon-"fa fa—reply "> <%= number_with_delimiters(post.comments_count) %> replies</a> 
    <p data-toggle  =   collapse', role   =    'button' ,data - target    = '#collapseablecommentsection ', title =     '"Expand Comment Section"'.html_safe, class =  ''> <i  clas s= f a—plus "></i> Add comment</a> 
    <div id=  post.id %}-comments # comments-container collapse   # do not forget to add :style   => 'height:    0; overflow:'auto' in the CSS section for this too -->> 
   <%= render partial: "post_comment", collection: [Comment, Comment], as: => comment if post.respond_to?(:comments) %> 
  </div>  
<!-- Add your custom content below-- >   
 </div> <br / >
